claim: Deep agents use the ReAct algorithm (LLM call → decide to stop or act → get feedback → repeat) implemented via LangGraph's create_react_agent
actionable: Use LangGraph's create_react_agent as the foundation for deep agents instead of building custom agent loops from scratch

claim: Agent state should track messages (human, AI, tool results), iteration count (last_step, remaining_steps), and task-specific data (to-dos, files)
actionable: Design your agent state schema to include all information the agent needs across turns (messages, memory, task context) in one immutable object

claim: Virtual file systems (dictionaries in state) are better than real file systems for agents because they're easier to parallelize and merge
actionable: Store agent-created files in state as a dictionary (path → content) instead of writing to disk; use file reducers to merge concurrent writes

claim: Tool descriptions are as important as function signatures; detailed descriptions (140-180 lines) with examples and constraints significantly improve LLM tool use
actionable: Spend time writing comprehensive tool descriptions with examples of when to use/not use each tool; this directly impacts agent reliability

claim: The write_todos planning tool overwrites the entire to-do list each time (not partial updates) because it helps agents remember and manage the full scope
actionable: When implementing agent planning tools, have agents generate and return the complete updated plan each time, not just deltas

claim: Read file tool should support line offsets and limits (not just return entire files) to keep context windows manageable
actionable: Implement read_file with optional start_line and end_line parameters; default to first 2000 lines to avoid overwhelming the model

claim: Edit file tool uses exact string replacement (not line numbers) because Claude models are trained to use string replacement natively
actionable: Implement edit_file with old_string → new_string replacement and a replace_all flag instead of line-number-based editing

claim: Sub-agents should only see one user message (the delegated task), not the full conversation history, to keep them focused
actionable: When delegating to a sub-agent, reset the message history to just the task description; sub-agents should inherit files/state but not conversation context

claim: Sub-agent tool returns only the final message (not all work), so prompts should instruct sub-agents to summarize all findings in their last response
actionable: When calling sub-agents, instruct them to include all relevant findings and results in their final message; intermediate work won't be passed back

claim: Sub-agent tool can parallelize multiple agent invocations; file reducer merges outputs automatically when agents write different files
actionable: Use parallel sub-agent calls for independent tasks (e.g., research + code generation); rely on the file merger to combine outputs

claim: Claude Sonnet with high max_tokens (for long outputs) is the default model for deep agents because research/writing tasks often generate long responses
actionable: When building deep agents for writing-heavy tasks, use Claude Sonnet with max_tokens set to the highest available value
